name: naiveproxy-client
note: Apache-2.0. Upstream NaiveProxy client binary, bundled in the panel image at /usr/local/bin/naive — the SAME upstream build as the server-side binary in the ct-naive container (v0.3.0+ invariant). Canonical pin lives in manifests/naive.upstream.json; docker/panel/Dockerfile's ARG defaults are auto-managed by operator/sync-naive-pin.ts and verified by `ct update` preflight + `make check-naive-pin`. The anti-tracking probe in core/ct-server-core/src/probe.rs spawns this binary as a child process bound to a free 127.0.0.1 port and dials through it as an HTTP CONNECT proxy. Using the upstream reference client guarantees wire-level compatibility with the server-side naive on the other end — that's the v0.3.0 architecture cut's whole point (replaced v0.2.x's Caddy `forwardproxy@naive` plugin, which had drifted from the upstream padding protocol). Verify command runs the binary, not just `test -x`, so Alpine/glibc loader regressions fail the component check before the Layer 4 probe is needed. Pin matches the actual `/usr/local/bin/naive --version` output (`naive 148.0.7778.96`); the upstream asset tag carries a `-5` rebuild suffix (bumped from `-2` in v0.0.89 after upstream deleted the `-2` release assets — same binary version 148.0.7778.96, different rebuilt tarball with the same `naive --version` line), but the installed binary does not print that suffix.
